events.name: Chicken Katsu & Sourdough Doughnuts
events.venue: 114 Middleton Rd, Churton Park, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
**Tomorrow's hot dish is chicken katsu**
What to expect at Wednesday's sale outside The Animal Medical Centre. five flavours of sourdough doughnuts, sourdough cinnamon buns, and hot, crispy, aromatic chicken, rice pilaf, vegetable slaw and sauces.
Every Wednesday afternoon, The Animal Medical Centre and Family Kitchen with Mark team up to serve hot, tasty, family-friendly meals and freshly made, slowly fermented sourdough doughnuts.
Set up in a gazebo outside the clinic, our portions are generous, the food is freshly cooked and seasonal, the wait is short (LESS THAN 5 MINUTES), and nothing is too spicy, making it perfect for everyone. Products can be pre-ordered through our website or picked up on the day. If you cannot pick up during these times, contact Mark to arrange to collect from our kitchen in Paparangi after 6 pm.
We plan the menu and update our website on a Monday or Tuesday.
All our dishes are served mild in spice levels.
Doughnuts can be pre-ordered through our website or picked up on the day.
https://familykitchenwithmark.co.nz/weekly-menu/
Eftpos is available at all our sales.

events.id: & Juliet-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: & Juliet
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
What might have happened if Shakespeare’s Romeo and Juliet had ended differently? A ‘what if’ scenario is given life in a new musical coming to Wellington in 2026. & Juliet explores the idea of what the Bard’s heroine might have done if she’d lived beyond the end of the play. The bold reimagining uses fantastic songs to tell a story through a fiercely independent lens. The result is a fabulous, feel-good journey of self-discovery, empowerment, and second chances. 
& Juliet is a jukebox musical. That means the story is told using a playlist of pop bangers you already know and love. It features chart-toppers like Since U Been Gone, Roar, Baby One More Time, Larger Than Life, and Can’t Stop the Feeling! The show pulses with energy, heart, and non-stop fun. 
You’ll be wowed with dazzling costumes, electrifying choreography, and hit after hit. The retelling of a classic love story through a bold new lens makes & Juliet more than just a musical. It’s a celebration of rewriting your own narrative.

events.id: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.id: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026
events.long_description:
What’s tough, fast, and physical? Wellington’s netball team, Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse. After reaching the semi-finals last year, the Pulse are hungry to dominate the ANZ Premiership this year. Their campaign kicks off in April and continues through to June. Five of the ten games are played on home soil, either at the TSB Arena on Wellington’s waterfront, Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua or Fly Palmy Arena in Palmerston North. 
Netball is known for its speed, agility, deft footwork, and pinpoint feeds. As a live spectator sport, it always delivers. This year, Khiarna Williams, Ameliaranne Ekenasio and Ine-Marí Venter provide the fireworks at the shooting end of the court. Head court-side to cheer them on and feel the energy pulse.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour-10 Kent Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour
events.venue: 10 Kent Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banff Mountain Film Festival World Tour presented by the NZ Alpine Club
The New Zealand Alpine Club with Presenting Partners, World Expeditions, La Sportiva, NZ Mountain Safety Council and Osprey, bring the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour back to Tāhuna Queenstown this April! Banff Centre Mountain Film and Book Festival is one of the largest and most prestigious mountain festivals in the world! Hot on the heels of the Festival that is held every fall in beautiful Banff, Alberta, the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour hits the road. With stops planned in over 600 communities and more than 40 countries across the globe, the Banff World Tour celebrates amazing achievements in outdoor storytelling and filmmaking worldwide. From the over 400 entries submitted to the Festival competition each year, award-winners and audience favourites are among the films that are carefully selected to play in theatres around the world. Travelling to remote vistas, analysing topical environmental issues, and bringing audiences up-close and personal with adrenaline-packed action sports, the 2025/2026 World Tour is an exhilarating and provocative exploration of the mountain world.
Be moved. Be inspired. Don’t miss out. Reserve your tickets today. For more information visit banff.nz and follow our Facebook page (LINK) for updates.
This screening is a fundraiser for the New Zealand Alpine Club.
We will be screening two programmes, Ruapehu and Tasman over two nights. More details are available here: https://banff.nz/faqs/

events.id: Nameless and Brainless-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Nameless and Brainless
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
***THIS SHOW IS KOHA ENTRY***
FIRST ACT: 6:30pm
6 piece Soul/Hip Hop Wellington band play Moon for the first time with support from Sam Fowles (solo) and Lucy Summerfield (solo with cello accompanied)
NAMELESS AND BRAINLESS:
What started as some jams for a Master's level project between a couple of the bros has evolved into a soulful, hard-hitting band.
While this project is fresh, each of the musicians either has a degree in music or has vast experience in the industry.
High-energy Kiwi raps in English and Te Reo, accompanied by a powerful band and driven by a locked-in rhythm section.
LUCY SUMMERFIELD:
Lucy Summerfield’s music is threaded through with intention. She explores the full range of human experience in her work with her poignant lyrics. The way she weaves these together with carefully crafted melodies has seen her recognised nationally for her songwriting in both te reo Māori and English.
She is joined onstage by talented cellist Joanna Dann (Boxhill Quartet).
SAM FOWLES:
Sam Fowles is a soul-searcher and sonic adventurer from Pōneke, Aotearoa. His music drifts from smoky, blues-drenched sunrises into funk-filled skies, weaving a tapestry of soulful psychedelia that moves both body and soul.
Live and solo, Sam becomes a guitar-slinging troubadour, wringing the truth from every phrase. Without the full band, the focus falls on his storytelling, the groove in his right hand, and the emotion in his voice. It’s soulful, intimate, and full of heart, showcasing a different side of his music while remaining unmistakably Sam
namelessandbrainless.com

events.id: Bear's Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bear's Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa. They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May. Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday
Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade. Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically. Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying.
Their emotive story telling and cinematic sound has made up a catalogue that translates into film and onto live stages, taking them to some of the world’s biggest arenas and festivals, including Reading and Leeds, Glastonbury Festival, Pukkelpop, Lowlands and Bonnaroo.
Consisting of founding member Andrew Davie on vocals and guitar, Christof van der Ven (banjo, guitar, vocals), Marcus Hamblett (synths, horns), and Jools Owen (drums, horns), the band have been on the road celebrating the 10 year anniversary of Islands for the past year and they’re now set to bring it all to New Zealand for the first time.

events.id: Terror-Fi Presents: OBSESSION (Advance Preview - Wellington)-12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Terror-Fi Presents: OBSESSION (Advance Preview - Wellington)
events.venue: 12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
You’re gonna fall in love with this film … hard! Be one of the first to see OBSESSION … the wickedly twisted new love story horror from Curry Barker (‘Milk & Serial’).
96% on Rotten Tomatoes, with delightfully warped performances from Inde Navarrette and Michael Johnston, you couldn’t wish for a stronger contender for ‘Best Horror of 2026’!
Come early to enjoy our themed lobby, limited-edition cocktail, and custom pre-show.
-- After breaking the mysterious "One Wish Willow" to win his crush's heart, a hopeless romantic finds himself getting exactly what he asked for but soon discovers that some desires come at a dark, sinister price. --
Directed by Curry Barker, the film stars Michael Johnston (‘Slash’), Inde Navarrette (‘Superman & Lois’), Cooper Tomlinson, Megan Lawless, and Andy Richter.

events.id: Tai Chi-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Tai Chi
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tai Chi
Instruction is given in 8 Yang-style forms and you will be able to perform these forms with confidence.  You'll also gain a good understanding of the martial applications and health benefits involved.
By the end of the course, you'll have had an introduction to this martial art and will: 
• Be able to demonstrate the Yang-style forms
• Have enhanced suppleness
• Have better balance
• Have better concentration
• Have practised exercises for enhanced blood circulation and energy
Wear loose, comfortable clothing and bring a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.description:
British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa.
They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May.
Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade.
Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically.
Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying.

events.id: Brynzilian Big Band-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Brynzilian Big Band
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Brynzilian Big Band is a 17-piece powerhouse jazz orchestra founded and led by saxophonist Bryn van Vliet. Based in Wellington, Bryn is a multi-disciplinary artist known for his dynamic career as an instrumentalist (saxophone, woodwinds, vocals), performer, composer, arranger, and educator. As a founding member of TOI and former lead alto saxophonist for the Rodger Fox Big Band, he has earned a reputation as a versatile and sought-after musician.

The Brynzilian Big Band presents fresh, vibrant compositions inspired by iconic Brazilian music styles such as Samba, Bossa Nova, and Forró. Bryn’s writing reflects his deep affection for Brazilian music, while honoring its traditional roots and spirit. Featuring 17 of Wellington’s finest musicians, the Brynzilian Big Band promises a performance that will make you dance, laugh, shout, cry — and maybe even hug your neighbour.

Music from 9pm, $20 on. the door $10 for students

Location: Badminton Wellington Centre

High-energy team competition

Entries via Venue Pro

events.id: Georgia Knight | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Georgia Knight | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way. After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New
Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band. Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges. Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai. 
It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia. Her independently released debut album Beanpole, described by KEXP as "an evocative set of dimly-lit, hypnotic, psych-tinted pop", earned praise from Sharon Van Etten, Arab Strap, and BBC Radio 6 Music. Critics highlighted her "shadowy, seductive trip-hop textures" (KCRW), "extraordinary vocal range and graveside poems" (The 13th Floor), and "goosebumps"-inducing live performances (BBC Radio 6). 
Supporting the album, she was booked for a string of highly coveted festivals including The Great Escape (UK), Golden Plains (AU), Phoenix Central Park (AU), The Others Way (NZ), and Port Noise (NZ). 
Now touring her 2025 album Beanpole, recorded with Andrew ‘Idge’ Hehir, Georgia returns with a fuller, immersive live show, intimate, cinematic, and impossible to look away from.

San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.
events.description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way.
After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.
Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges.
Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai.
It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia.

events.long_description:
Celebrate Wellington's longest-awaited new bike route with us on opening day, Saturday 16 May.
Route: Waterfront, stadium concourse to Thorndon Quay, Old Hutt Road to Ngauranga to join the new path, exiting at Honiara Reserve in Petone. Return via the same route.
BYO picnic - details TBD, either at the reserve or one of the Ūranga (stopping points) on the way back.
We'll ride at a steady pace, not too fast. For anyone who doesn't want to ride back, trains to WLG leave Petone station every half hour - but expect some waiting if lots of other folks have the same idea!
10:15am Meet under the sails at Queens Wharf on the waterfront

11:30pm onwards: Check out the NZTA event at Pito-One, then picnic and return ride following the same route.

events.id: Forest giants, fragrant herbs and birdsong - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Forest giants, fragrant herbs and birdsong - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
In this 75-minute walk we will get up close to some delightful pygmy pines before, on a gentle slope, passing under and being awed by their towering cousins en route to a wonderful view of Wellington City.
In the Herb Garden we will see and hear a little about the aromatic, culinary and medicinal properties of familiar and not-so-familiar herbs, before descending, possibly accompanied by some birdsong, through a tract of native forest down to Lady Norwood Rose Garden.
Meet at the Founders’ Entrance, Glenmore Street.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Multi-award-winning Kiwi comic, radio presenter and TV personality Hayley Sproull is back in her hometown. She has a brand-new show that’s not safe for on-air. In Sproull On the Prowl, the self-described walking tornado (over)shares about a wild 12 months. It features stifled farts, hall passes, and an exploration of the Indonesian bush with a cameo from Jesus. 
The Toi Whakaari graduate has come a long way since bursting onto the comedy scene in 2012 when she was named Best Newcomer at that year’s International Comedy Festival. She’s made herself a regular on our airwaves since then through her breakfast radio show and appearances on Taskmaster, 7 Days, Have You Been Paying Attention? and The Great Kiwi Bake Off. Make sure you leave the kids at home for what promises to be a wild night.

events.id: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
The ultimate class in practical cheese making!   Learn all you need to know to replicate making NZ style Brie and Queso Fresco at home.  You will be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of Brie. Then, while the Brie is ripening and setting (don't worry, all terminology will be explained!), we will make Queso Fresco together.  
All ingredients are provided as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, a dishcloth, 3 or 4 tea towels, a pen to take notes and a 2 litre container and plastic re-sealable food bags to take your cheese home.
During the workshop, the following topics will also be covered: Milk and where to source it, hygiene and sanitation, equipment required and the origins of the cheeses you are about to make.  You will also receive comprehensive instruction on how to mature your Brie cheese over the next few weeks.
A small break will be provided and you will have the opportunity to purchase equipment and cheesemaking ingredients after the workshop should you wish to replicate the experience at home.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Make Brie and Queso Fresco
- Recall the best milk to use and where to source it from
- Name and identify what equipment and ingredients you need to make Brie and Queso Fresco
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
Love Singapore Hawker food? Come and discover how to recreate some of these dishes from local ingredients with Mei-Fen, who is passionate about Asian food. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.   NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Prepare a range of Singapore “hawker” style dishes which may Kueh Dadar, Mee Goreng and Laksa
• Follow the recipes provided and prepare them with supervision
• Prepare the recipes from local ingredients
• Identify retail outlets which supply the specialty ingredients required 
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Meet the demand for excellence in the job market head on. Learn how to utilise the STARR technique in the way that recruiters use it. That is, to define and seek out the skills and qualities of the best candidate. Master the STARR skills and feel confident in your next job interview.

Familiarise yourself with the skills needed to ace your next interview. Learn the STARR method as an approach to responding to behavioural based questions. Understand how to use concrete examples from your life as proof that you possess the experience and skills for the job you are applying for. You’ll develop the key interview skill of being able to share your accomplishments through real-life examples.

In this course, you are taught how to identify specific example situations that are unique to you. You’ll learn how to confidently talk about the tasks involved and describe how you completed the key task and met its challenges. We’ll also consider the context of jobs you are interviewing for and how to choose examples that best show the desired skills interviewers are seeking.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Develop a higher level of confidence in approaching job interviews.
- Identify the different components of the STARR acronym and apply them to the job interview context.
- Identify what behavioural based questions are and why recruiters use them.
- Successfully answer behavioural based interview questions by applying the STARR technique.
- Develop skills in using real life examples to show transferable skills in the workplace.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from ChatGPT
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools like ChatGPT are everywhere, but how do you get the best out of them? In this hands-on, phone-based workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make ChatGPT a more useful, reliable partner in your work and life. Whether you’ve tried it a few times or use it regularly, you’ll learn how to write better prompts, refine responses, and avoid common pitfalls without needing any technical background.
We’ll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together so you leave feeling confident and ready to put your new skills to use.
Please bring: A phone with the ChatGPT app installed, and enough battery to last the day.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
Use ChatGPT for research, brainstorming, and creative work
Recognise the tool’s limitations (and how to work around them)
Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
Confidently troubleshoot when ChatGPT gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Retirement Reinvented
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Retirement Reinvented
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.
It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the day, you’ll have:
Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your values and your strengths
Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explore choices that can provide you with that sense of purpose
Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Begun to develop your blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and have considered a “future proofing” downsizing plan.
Bring pen and paper.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Reconnect with yourself, improve your communication, and create more ease in your relationships
Come and spend a restorative day on a beautiful rural property in Makara Valley, just 20 minutes from Wellington City (10 minutes from Karori).
This retreat is an invitation to slow down, step away from the noise of everyday life, and reconnect with yourself, with nature, and with a small group of like-minded people.
Understanding your relationship with yourself and how this shapes the way you connect with others. Through guided discussions and self-reflection exercises, you’ll gain insight into self-talk, self-love, your inner and outer worlds, and the ways we mirror what’s happening within us.
We’ll also explore relationships with others, including attachment styles, communication, boundaries, intentions, and simple, practical tools to create more ease, alignment, and connection in your life.
This learning is gently woven into a relaxing and restorative day. You’ll be supported through a guided meditation, cacao, a reflective nature exercise, yin yoga (gentle stretching and reconnecting with your body), a calming sound bath, and time to rest and unwind.
Your day will include:
– 10am -Welcome cacao, introductions, and a guided meditation
– A workshop exploring your relationship with yourself
– A reflective nature walk with guided prompts
– Group sharing and reflection
– Wholesome vegetarian lunch and time to relax (including use of the spa)
– A workshop on relationships and effective communication
– Gentle yin yoga to support integration through the body
– A healing sound bath
– A closing circle to reflect and take your insights forward
Spaces are limited, only 8 spaces available.
About your facilitator:
Briar is an experienced communication coach supporting mums going through family separation with qualifications in conflict coaching she is also a registered Circle of Security Parenting Programme facilitator (a programme helping parents create healthy attachment and connection with thier children). Briar brings a grounded, compassionate approach to supporting people in understanding themselves and their relationships.
Michelle will be running the yin yoga session, a slow-paced, meditative style of yoga characterized by passive floor poses and gentle stretches allowing you to integrate lessons and reconnect with yourself. Michelle is a versatile and seasoned yoga teacher, her classes are a blend of creativity, intuition, and safety - offering a nurturing space for curiosity and deep nourishment.
Investment:
Early bird: $111 (for bookings before 1st May)
Standard price: $155.
Text or call for more information and details on how to find the venue
027 366 3273.
Bring a yoga mat (have a few spare if needed), journal, drink bottle and open heart

events.id: SLOMOSA - YEAR OF THE HORSE TOUR - W/ SIDEWINDER & MAMMUTHUS-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: SLOMOSA - YEAR OF THE HORSE TOUR - W/ SIDEWINDER & MAMMUTHUS
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
SLOMOSA ARE COMING TO NEW ZEALAND!
Hailing from the city of Bergen, Norway, Slomosa channel the sprawl of their surroundings through a sweeping signature hybrid of revved-up stoner rock riffage, grungy hooks, and a concentrated punk wallop. As if holding up a sonic mirror image to the landscape, an avalanche of distortion tumbles into valleys of massive melody, teeming with tectonic force. Now, the group—Ben Berdous [vocals, guitar], Marie Moe [vocals, bass], Tor Erik Bye [guitar], and Jard Hole [drums]—perfect this inimitable sound on their aptly titled second full-length offering and MNRK Heavy debut LP, Tundra Rock.
Joining them on the tour are NZ veteran stoner band Sidewinder.
Since forming in late 2020, the band has carved out a formidable presence in Aotearoa’s heavy music scene with their signature blend of riff-heavy psych, swampy delta blues, and southern stoner rock. 2026 will see the release of their third album - Dominion.
In Auckland they are also joined by Tusk
In Taupo by The Bums
And in Wellington by Mammuthus
Tickets via UTR

events.id: C-26-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: C-26
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
First gig for C-26 in 2026 at Rogue and Vagabond. Just $10 at the door or online. We will be there from 9:30 pm. Bring your friends and celebrate with some cool salsa tunes. Spread the love and see you there.

C-26 is a sizzling seven piece salsa band from Wellington. They are a great mix of South American and Kiwi professional musicians who bring vibrant and energetic music to the Wellington scene.

Their sound and style is inspired by the salsa classics from Cuba Puerto Rico and Colombia. Featuring the first female Latino salsa singer in Wellington, Anayibi Loboa from Colombia, C-26 is the Salsa purist's band, fully live, with no backing tracks, C-26 produces a sound and feel built from the classical combination of horns, timbales, congas, guiro, trumpet, saxophone, piano and bass that defines the authentic salsa experience.

Music from 9:30pm, tickets from Hummanitix

events.id: Pōneke House Party | NZICF 26-Manners Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Pōneke House Party | NZICF 26
events.venue: Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
A MASSIVE NIGHT OF JOKES AND SONGS AND MORE JOKES
One night. One iconic room. Comedy and music, together at last. The NZ International Comedy Festival and NZ Music Month Te Marama Puoro o Aotearoa present Pōneke House Party, a massive night of jokes, songs and surprise special guests at Wellington’s Opera House, Sunday 17 May, 6pm.
Co-headlined by Topp Prize-winning comedy great, Tofiga Fepulea'i, and country music’s finest, Tami Neilson, this is a full-scale celebration of Aotearoa’s comedy and music scenes, sharing the same stage for one loud, loose, good-spirited night where anything can happen.
Hosted by James Nokise, and held together by house band The Funkadelics, this once in a lifetime show also features comedians Justine Smith (Taskmaster), Michele A’Court (7 Days), Rhys Mathewson (7 Days); musical comedians Wilson Dixon (Fred Award winner, 2010) and Karen O’Leary (Wellington Paranormal); musicians Samuel Flynn Scott and Luke Buda (The Phoenix Foundation), rising opera/RnB talent LILA JNR (World of WearableArt) and indie rock four-piece Dropper. Many more acts and special surprise guests to come.
Expect comedy, music, comedy and music, tap-dancing, saxophones in the spotlight, and more. Much more.
All profits from the event will be donated to the Mayoral Flood Relief Fund, supporting those impacted by the recent flooding in Te Whanganui-a-Tara.

events.id: The Day the Earth Stood Still-The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Roxy Cinema
events.name: The Day the Earth Stood Still
events.venue: The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Details
Directed by Robert Wise
Starring Michael Rennie, Hugh Marlowe, Patricia Neal
--- When a UFO lands in Washington, D.C., bearing a message for Earth's leaders, all of humanity stands still. Klaatu has come on behalf of alien life who have been watching Cold War-era nuclear proliferation on Earth. But it is Klaatu's soft-spoken robot Gort
that presents a more immediate threat to onlookers. A single mother and her son teach the world about peace and tolerance in this moral fable, ousting the tanks and soldiers that greet the alien's arrival. ---

This is a TEA TIME TALKIE screening where a delicious Devonshire Tea* awaits for upgraded tickets holders (a cup of tea and traditional homemade scones with cream and jam). This is served upstairs in the Grand Lobby at the intermission.

Admit only tickets are available for $15 or you can upgrade to include a Devonshire Tea for $24.

*No dietaries catered for.

Presented in 4K

Schedule:
2:35pm – Cinema doors open
2:45pm - Feature starts
3:30pm - Intermission / Devonshire Tea available for collection upstairs in the Grand Lobby
3:45pm – Feature resumes

events.id: Introduction to Nichiren Buddhism: 5-week course-287 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Introduction to Nichiren Buddhism: 5-week course
events.venue: 287 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hi!

Join us for a free 5-week introductory course where you can learn about the teachings of Nichiren Buddhism and the practice of chanting Nam-myoho-renge-kyo
Tuesdays 7–8pm from 5 May to 2 June
You’re welcome to join all 5 sessions or just pop in for 1–2!
Everyone is welcome to join!
At this introductory course you’ll have the opportunity to learn about Buddhist concepts, opportunities to ask any questions and, hear people share their experiences of this Buddhist practice.
Topics include

5 May – Why do we chant Nam-myoho-renge-kyo and what does it mean?
12 May – Who is a Buddha - Our potential for Buddhahood and other human conditions
19 May – The Gohonzon - the mirror of our enlightened life
26 May – Changing our desitiny and the oneness of self and the environment
2 June – changing the world through inner transformation
Don’t miss this opportunity to explore the teachings of Nichiren Buddhism and take a step toward a more fulfilling life
We look forward to seeing you there!
Feel free to message us with any questions
About Us:
The Soka Gakkai International (SGI) is a community-based Buddhist organisation promoting peace, culture, and education, centred on respect for the dignity of life. With members in 192 countries and territories
SGI members strive to bring out their potential while contributing as empowered global citizens to their communities
Whether you’re new to Buddhism or have some experience, our SGI community offers a warm, supportive space to explore this philosophy and discover a path to inner transformation.
Nam-myoho-renge-kyo!

events.id: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
THE WATERBOYS make their long-awaited return to New Zealand in May 2026 their first tour in over a decade!
Critically acclaimed as one of the best live bands on the planet, the 2025 band features double keyboard players Brother Paul from Memphis and James Hallawell from Cornwall and is grounded by killer Irish rhythm section Aongus Ralston (bass) and Eamon Ferris (drums).
THE WATERBOYS will be performing tracks from their audacious new album, Life, Death and Dennis Hopper: a bold, cinematic tribute to the maverick American actor and cultural icon, along with material across their storied catalogue. From the early "big music" of The Whole Of The Moon and classic album This Is The Sea through to the roots-inspired Fisherman's Blues era and music from their ambitious post-2015 output.
Mike Scott comments: "The Waterboys live for the experience of playing, of feeling our music evolve and grow with each consecutive concert. Nothing makes us happier than seeing a long list of dates like these. How will the music change? What will happen that's never happened before? How much fun and thrills can we and the audiences have? Australia and New Zealand come and join us!"

events.id: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society - Pop-up-1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society - Pop-up
events.venue: 1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Port of call pop-up shanty show to celebrate the release of our new EP.
5pm. Left Bank Arcade off Cuba st. Free!
The wave doesn’t conform to the shape of the rock. It smashes it. The sea doesn’t pretend to be hospitable. It summons courage. And a shantyband never licks the captain's boot. They release another EP!
The best shanties invite us to open our hearts along with the bottle. To draw strength from camaraderie and drown our pettinesses. To seek the rough edge of the wave and savour the salt within. And so, the WSSS have issued a call to arms with their new collection: Never Mind the Rowlocks, Here’s the Wellington Sea Shanty Society.
Never fear if you find yourself adrift on a sea of AI slop, lapping at your hull, lulling you to sleep — Lake Davineer, Vorn dont Le Père Était Marin, Young Nic, Tomas el Largo, and Louis Quatorze are back again to fill your sails and slap the codfish slime clean off your cheeks.
Just when everyone is lamenting the empty galleys of the good ship Wellington, and those ol’ scurvy-mongers are promising renewed bounty if you would only tack five degrees to port or starboard come election time, these songs remind us the sea at least will sustain us, and there’s rum and (metaphorical) guns for them that wants to fight.
Lin Seal | Wellington Nautical History Monthly

events.id: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting at Percy Scenic Reserve-Western Hutt Rd, Lower Hutt-Facebook
events.name: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting at Percy Scenic Reserve
events.venue: Western Hutt Rd, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Explore your local awa with freshwater experts from our Wai Connection team at this spotlighting event at Percy Scenic Reserve. With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi to your local waterway to ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. Learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.
Book a spot here: https://events.humanitix.com/awa-after-dark-spotlighting...
Outdoor event: This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
Location: The streams we spotlight often don't have an 'address'. Before the event, we'll email you all the details you need to find our exact meeting location on the night.
events.description:
With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi to your local waterway to ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat.
Learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
Before the event, we'll email you all the details you need to find our exact meeting location on the night.

events.id: Welly On Wheels Guided Ride: Stargazing Edition-12 Queens Whrf, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Welly On Wheels Guided Ride: Stargazing Edition
events.venue: 12 Queens Whrf, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
See the bays in a whole new light with this special evening ride.
Join us for a unique night-time bike ride around the bays and Kilbirnie, where we’ll explore the exciting street changes and improvements in the area while enjoying a bit of stargazing!
This special ride is hosted by Switched on Bikes, GWRC Pedal Ready, and the Wellington Astronomical Society. It’s a fantastic way to gain confidence on your bike, learn about new infrastructure, and discover how fun it can be to travel by bike at night. We’ll stop at the Moai Statue in Lyall Bay, where the Wellington Astronomical Society will have telescopes set up for some awesome stargazing; a crescent moon and Jupiter are due to be visible!
Event details
Meeting point: SwitchedOn Bikes on the waterfront
Date: Thursday 21 May
Bike collection: 5:00pm
Ride start: 5:30pm
Ride finish: 8:00pm
Tickets: $20
Each ticket includes an e-bike hire and will be refunded if you have your own e-bike.

events.id: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s a world-renowned guitarist and master entertainer. Tommy Emmanuel, CGP, is bringing his beloved Maton guitar to The Opera House stage in Wellington. He is considered a living legend in the acoustic guitar world. The Australian musical legend has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling technique. The concert promises to be an inspiring journey as Tommy strums and plucks his way through jazz, rock, country and pop music. Fans will be captivated by the diverse musical landscape he creates. 
Since his career began in the 1960s, Tommy has toured with luminaries like Eric Clapton and John Denver. He has won a Grammy Award and performed for a televised audience of more than two billion at the 2000 Sydney Olympics. Collaborations include guitar greats like Les Paul, Mark Knopfler, Joe Walsh, and Richard Thompson. 
Tommy’s hero, legendary guitarist Chet Atkins, bestowed the title of Certified Guitar Player (CGP) on him. He is one of only four players in the world with the title. Tommy Emmanuel CGP continues to push the boundaries of what is possible on the acoustic guitar.
events.description:
He’s a world-renowned guitarist and master entertainer.
He is considered a living legend in the acoustic guitar world.
The Australian musical legend has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling technique.
The concert promises to be an inspiring journey as Tommy strums and plucks his way through jazz, rock, country and pop music.
Tommy Emmanuel CGP continues to push the boundaries of what is possible on the acoustic guitar.

events.id: Titan - Wellington-111 Wakefield St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Titan - Wellington
events.venue: 111 Wakefield St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Violinist James Ehnes performs Korngold’s dazzling, brilliant Violin Concerto before NZSO Artistic Advisor and Principal Conductor Gemma New leads the orchestra in Mahler’s magnificent Titan symphony.
Programme:
Thomas Adès The Origin Of The Harp (NZ Premiere)
Korngold Violin Concerto
Mahler Symphony No. 1, Titan
We begin in a mythical world, where a sea nymph is transformed in Thomas Adès’ haunting and atmospheric The Origin of the Harp.
From such exquisitely misty transfiguration, we emerge into the dazzling, technicolour brilliance of Korngold’s Violin Concerto. Performed by James Ehnes, Gramophone’s 2021 Artist of the Year, his extraordinary spectrum of colour and articulation finds its perfect match in this beautiful neo-Romantic score.
At the time of writing his first symphony, Mahler was best known as a conductor. Titan emphatically announced his arrival as a composer of extraordinary talent, bursting, sun-like, above the horizon. From primordial strings, to clarinet cuckoo calls heralding a sumptuous dawn, to the work’s mercurial moods of joy, gloom, hell and heaven, this magnificent symphony is the sound of the boundless universe itself being born.
Generously supported by Bill Falconer CNZM.
events.description:
Violinist James Ehnes performs Korngold’s dazzling, brilliant Violin Concerto before NZSO Artistic Advisor and Principal Conductor Gemma New leads the orchestra in Mahler’s magnificent Titan symphony.
Programme: Thomas Adès The Origin Of The Harp (NZ Premiere) Korngold Violin Concerto Mahler Symphony No.
At the time of writing his first symphony, Mahler was best known as a conductor.
Titan emphatically announced his arrival as a composer of extraordinary talent, bursting, sun-like, above the horizon.
From primordial strings, to clarinet cuckoo calls heralding a sumptuous dawn, to the work’s mercurial moods of joy, gloom, hell and heaven, this magnificent symphony is the sound of the boundless universe itself being born.

events.id: DONAL GUNNING & THE BUCKS - AUTUMN TOUR-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: DONAL GUNNING & THE BUCKS - AUTUMN TOUR
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
By popular demand Dónal Gunning proudly announces the Autumn ‘Don’t Give a Buck Tour’. Leaving Dublin in November 2022 for New Zealand in pursuit of adventure and a full-time music career, his fans are hungry to see him and his band The Bucks live once again.
Following a breakout 2025, his sophomore EP, Zero Bucks Given was exceedingly well received across the globe—earning international radio play, landing prominent playlists, and drawing high praise from critics. The EP catapulted Dónal’s streams and monthly listeners to an all-time high, showcasing a powerful evolution in his sound and songwriting. The release featured a triumphant 15 date tour of New Zealand.
The Irishman is going from strength to strength in 2026, his debut album set to drop this winter. The Don’t Give a Buck Autumn tour promoting the singles off the upcoming album plus new material.
Dónal will be performing in Whanganui, Taupo, New Plymouth, Levin, Napier, Palmerston North, Wellington & Masterton. Joined by the mighty bucks Stan Shepherd (Guitar), Mason J Williams (Drums) & Jake Bergstrom (Guitar).
Tickets via UTR

events.id: Wednesday | Wellington w/ Mudgoose -San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Wednesday | Wellington w/ Mudgoose
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
When North Carolina’s noise makers Wednesday first visited Aotearoa off the back of their album RAT SAW GOD, audiences were taken by surprise in the best possible way. A 95bFM review called the band’s Whammy show “a sold out countrygaze extravaganza”, and everyone who came left with big hearts and even bigger smiles. Now, with the band’s unbelievable sixth album Bleeds out in the world, it’s high time that Banished Music and Strange News had their fun pals from Asheville down for a repeat visit, with three shows across Wellington, Lyttelton, and Auckland happening this May.

“Rock records don’t come much better than this” - NME ***** 

Bleeds is the album the band are calling “not only the best Wednesday record—it’s also the most Wednesday record”, with all the elements that make the Southern rock magic of this rare group so special. NME put it beautifully in their five-star review of the album, heaping well deserved praise on Wednesday’s “blown-out distortion, weirdo-country slide guitar and serrated melodies”. Fronted and founded by the fierce and fun Karly Hartzman, whose songs are at once confessional and crack-up, Wednesday have built a formidable reputation for being one of the best - and most dynamic - live bands on the circuit. Joining Karly will be the inimitable and animated Xandy Chelmis on lap steel and pedal steel, Alan Miller (drums), Ethan Baechtold (bass, piano).

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.id: Rafael And Hif Afrokan-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Rafael And Hif Afrokan
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Launched as a quintet at The Cross in December 2016, AfroKan* was relaunched as a septeto in October 2025 as part of the Wellington Jazz Festival to celebrate resident Cuban musician and musical director Rafael Ferrer Noel's 20th anniversary in New Zealand.

AfroKan plays 100% authentic and totally danceable Afro-Cuban and Latin Jazz with a bit of Salsa and Cha Cha Cha on top, and is a collaboration between resident Afro-Latino and top-end loca jazzl musicians..

Its repetoire includes compositions by Afro-Cuban Jazz greats such as Chucho Valdéz, Carlos “Patato” Valdéz, Poncho Sánchez, Rubén González and Orlando “Maraca” Valle amongst others.

Tonight's ensemble features:

- Rafael Ferrer Noel (Cuba) - vocals and congas

- Mikey Trujillo Rodriguez (Perú) - vocals and bongos

- John Rae (Scotland) - drums and timbales

- Patrick Bleakley (Wairarapa) - double bass

- Ayrton Foote (Wellington) - keyboard

- Daniel Windsor (Wellington) - trumpet

- Oscar Lavën (Wellington) - Tenor Saxophone

- Rosina van der Aa (Waiwhetu) - Manager

*AfroKan is a Yoruba term used in Cuba meaning Africano de Corazón – African at Heart.

Music from 930, FREE ENTRY - Koha for the band encouraged

events.id: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Michael Fowler Centre is set for two visits by the alt-country/folk musician and his band, The Yarra Benders. The tour is called Tā te Manawa — it literally means ‘heart at rest’. This references Marlon’s wish to take a break after a particularly busy and productive few years. 
Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening. The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’. The second delves into repertoire from his first album and bits of everything in between. Marlon explains the reason for the break, “For nearly 20 years I’ve explored both the physical and musical world in the company of incredible musicians, songwriters and friends. The catch with it is — it can be easy to overlook the toll it can take over time, on body and soul.” Fair not though, this is only a hiatus. Marlon describes it as a cheerful hurrah, “a see you soon of sorts.”

The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’.

events.id: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for some of the biggest anthems in the rock world. Rock Tenors roar into The Opera House in Wellington for a night of big tunes and big voices. The Anthems Reloaded Tour celebrates key moments in the Kiwi group’s musical journey. 
The five members of Rock Tenors have wide experience. They cross multiple music genres from opera and musical theatre to contemporary rock and thrash metal. They’ve reinvented the standard rock vocal arrangements with harmonic complexity, so audiences will get a dose of skilled musicality as well as nostalgia. You’ll hear The Rock Tenors’ signature renditions of legendary anthems from bands stretching back to the 60s. Hear Queen, The Animals, Bon Jovi, Aerosmith, AC/DC, Black Sabbath, Iron Maiden, White Snake, KISS, Guns N’ Roses, Red Hot Chilli Peppers, U2, and more. 
The touring ensemble of James Adams, Luke Butson, Ben Hayward, Steve Jones, and Justin Wilson are some of New Zealand’s premier vocalists. Since forming in 2022, they have taken the South Island by storm. They have become the must-see act for their vocal mastery and high-voltage rock singing.

events.id: Roller Derby - Home Brew Round 2-Wellington-Facebook

events.long_description:
Home Brew was such a success we’re back for another year! Join us for the first round in our 2026 Home Brew Series where the freshly brewed teams ‘Stone Cold Classics’ and ‘Craft Heads’ go helmet-to-helmet.
Home Brew was created to celebrate over 10 years of partnership between Richter City Roller Derby and the rad crew at Garage Project, who will be slinging brews rink-side.

events.id: Marlon Williams Tā te Manawa Tour | Wellington (SOLD OUT)-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Marlon Williams Tā te Manawa Tour | Wellington (SOLD OUT)
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Marlon Williams has today announced a very significant tour. It’s one that is truly for fans, and one that also marks a temporary departure from the stage for Marlon himself.
After an intensive time making latest album Te Whare Tīwekaweka and over two years filming the Ngā Ao E Rua - Two Worlds (2025) documentary Marlon has decided to take a sabbatical from the spotlight.
Rest and rejuvenation are on the horizon, but not before kiwi fans get to see him live for a cheerful hurrah, a see you soon of sorts…
Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders are warming up to bring it all home.
This is a heartfelt coming together of fans and whanau for Marlon. He invites you to travel through his musical repertoire to date and be in the room before he takes a step back from the stage…. for now.
Tickets go on sale Tuesday 24 Feb, midday.

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Microsoft Excel - Beginners
Learn how to create and use spreadsheets, as well as identify elements of a work book, enter and edit data, generate basic calculations and use built-in spreadsheet functions.
Windows skills essential - this is not a course for absolute beginners to computing.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had a basic introduction to using MS Excel, including how to:
• Identify elements of a workbook
• Page setup and printing
• Enter and edit data and basic formatting
• Generate basic calculations
• Use cell references and built-in functions 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: Strategy, Skills and Self-Awareness
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Equip yourself with the tools and strategies to thrive in today’s competitive job market. This course is designed to help job seekers at all stages across any industry. Whether you're applying for your first job, changing careers, or re-entering the workforce, this course is relevant for everyone because it requires you to self-reflect on your own journey. You’ll learn to reflect deeply on your goals and strengths while building practical skills to stand out from the crowd.
 You’ll leave with a set of practical tools, clear strategies, and a better understanding of what employers want, so you can step into your next opportunity with confidence.
 You’ll develop a tailored job search strategy that works for you. One that has purpose, highlights your transferable skills, sharpens your professional image, and helps you connect authentically with hiring managers. From crafting a digital-ready CV and setting job alerts, to working with recruiters and networking effectively, you’ll gain tools and insights to streamline the process.
 The course also addresses the emotional journey of job hunting. Learn to manage time and energy, stay adaptable, and build resilience as you face the highs and lows of searching for the right opportunity. You’ll explore how emotional intelligence, digital literacy, and critical thinking play essential roles in first impressions, interviews, and long-term career decisions.
 By the end of this course, you’ll be able to:
Identify and articulate your transferable skills.
Apply both emotional and intellectual strategies to mentor and coach yourself from within.
Develop a job search strategy that aligns with your goals.
Build confidence in presenting yourself professionally and authentically.
Develop a confident, authentic approach to your personal branding and professional networking.
Create and manage a job search plan using shortcuts like job alerts, recruitment agents, and focussed applications.
Maintain a polished digital presence and decide whether the use of professional platforms are for you (LinkedIn).
Understand how to navigate the emotional demands of job hunting with self-compassion, motivation, and adaptability.
Build and maintain a network of trusted referees who reflect your work ethic and potential.
 Whether you're starting from scratch or just need a refresh, this course offers a structured, encouraging path forward to help you land your next opportunity.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Love a piece of clothing but can't find a pattern?  Bring along clothes you want to take patterns off and learn some useful tips and sizing rules. You'll make a pattern for at least one garment and get help on how to get going with others.

events.id: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
Join us for a fun day of creature making! Design and sculpt a one-of-a-kind character in your own style from polymer clays in a full spectrum of colours!

Working with oven-cured clays and wrapped wire armatures, this workshop offers a variety of modelling methods and approaches - a great opportunity to turn sketches & ideas into tangible 3D forms. Modern clay inventions mean we can create translucent, glittery, semi-flexible, UV reactive or even glow-in-the-dark elements to add more realism or a touch of the surreal into each creation!

Students will be coached through designing and creating a small-scale creature sculpture, beginning simple, and building to a more complex final form as we get familiar with materials.

The class offers beginner students a fun introduction to sculpting organic forms from scratch, and for those with some hands-on creative experience, it offers a great opportunity to experiment, hone and advance your making skills to new levels.

If you can, bring along some design ideas, sketches or reference images to help kick start the sculpting process on the day.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
An interactive workshop presented by experienced physiotherapist Sara Dore, focusing on how movement and function can be challenging for women as they age. Particularly for women, there is more to the core than abdominal muscles and strength and we explore these broader aspects.  Most importantly, you will come to understand that movement is medicine and we can make a difference.
You will be able to make a positive difference for yourself! The objective of this workshop is to give you knowledge and understanding about how you can manage the effects on your health and wellbeing as we age as women.  Knowledge is power!
In this one-day workshop you will discover:
- The base of the pelvic floor. Anatomy of the pelvic floor, and the common conditions urinary incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse will be discussed. Education on basic pelvic floor advice and management as well as appropriate exercises will also be covered.
- The middle deep abdominal muscles. Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed. The principles of Clinical Pilates versus traditional Pilates will be presented together with the key principles of movement is medicine.
-The importance of the diaphragm and breathing well will also be discussed to enhance optimal movement and function for women.
Please wear comfortable clothes and socks as this is an interactive workshop, and bring a yoga mat or beach towel.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Writing Short Stories
In this one-day course, we’ll explore the elements that make short stories shine, from creating compelling character dynamics, to choosing the point of view that best serves your story.
Through a mix of discussions and practical writing exercises, participants will leave the course with all the tools they need to write engaging short stories.
Please bring pen and paper, and the name of a short story you like.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring your lunch and snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify elements that make short stories memorable
- Create compelling characters and relationship dynamics
- Write using different points of view
- Explore using symbolism in short fiction
- Use detail to develop setting and character traits
- Identify places to send your completed short stories
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
The date is confirmed.
Haere Mai Ki Te Fly Wellington Women’s Global event is confirmed for Sunday 24th May 2026 .
This event will be held at Harcourt Park if we reach greater than 45 entries we will be using California park also.
What is Haere Mai Ki Te Fly - it’s a disc golf event specifically for all female participants. New and current ladies and Girls all are welcome and encouraged to come have fun and play the event. The women’s global event happens eyelet 2 years so don’t put off til next time. This year it’s happening over 2 weekends would wide.
Great for all skill levels and a great opportunity for females of all ages to get out and be active, you never know you may get hooked.

https://www.discgolfscene.com/.../Haere_Mai_Ki_te_Fly_WGE...
Event cost:
Pro divisions - $40 (small event players pack included) with the option of purchasing the ammeter players pack.
Ammeter divisions - $45 (small event player pack + disc and towel
Combo)
Location: Upper Hutt
Date: Sunday 24th May 2026
Course: Harcourt park / California park
Rounds: Two rounds (18 holes) of disc golf will be played and one round for juniors under 12 years.

events.id: Heartland Flyer-Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: Heartland Flyer
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Travel behind steam with Steam Incorporated on a special day excursion to Woodville via the spectacular Manawatū Gorge, hauled by one of our majestic coal-fired steam locomotives.
Departing from Paekākāriki and picking up at Paraparaumu, Waikanae, Ōtaki, Levin, and Palmerston North, this journey takes you through a landscape normally hidden from view. With the Gorge road closed since 2017, rail passengers now enjoy the only way to experience this historic route and witness the dramatic slips that reshaped the valley.
At Woodville, step back in time at the restored 1960s-era station while the locomotive prepares for the return journey to Kāpiti. Look out across the ranges and you’ll also spot Meridian’s Te Āpiti Wind Farm, spanning 1,150 hectares and producing enough clean energy to power around 45,000 New Zealand homes.
Our Lounge Car, offering a selection of cool beverages, tea/coffee and some light snacks and food options will be open throughout the day. Please bring cash as the EFTPOS will be in and out of service and may not be available during parts of the trip due to remote locations. All alcohol must be consumed on board, no alcohol is to leave the train. BYO is prohibited. On board services are limited and while stock lasts!
Enjoy a relaxed day out to beat the Autumn blues, and enjoy a day onboard Steam Incorporated's heritage train.
Fares
Departing Paekakariki/Paraparaumu/Waikanae/Otaki/Levin
Adult 13+ yrs - $139
Child 2-12yrs - $99
Departing Palmerston North
Family Pass - $199
Adult 13+ yrs - $69
Child 2-12yrs - $49
Infants under 2 travel free of charge, provided they are on the knee of a full fare paying adult.

events.id: Dance4dance - A Fundraiser For Cancer Treatment-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Dance4dance - A Fundraiser For Cancer Treatment
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
We are hosting this gig to raise funds for a dear friend of mine’s mother, who is dealing with a very rare combination of stage 4 colon cancer with Braf mutation and breast cancer. We will be raising funds to go towards her treatment. Due to the rarity of this mutation, the $150k treatment is not funded by the government. She is a single mumma of three and is unable to work nor receive any government benefits (at the moment).


Thank you to the musicians who are giving up their talents for this cause:

Tui is a four-piece reggae band combining Pacific reggae and dub with introspective song themes tackling affairs within the Pacific. With a good deal of flourishing echoes and heavy drum and bass, Tui carries a meditative momentum that speaks on identity, colonial unrest and tradition. Tui is made up of drummer Harper Tewaru Champion, bassist Becky Devine, guitarist Timothy Le Cheminent-Reid and singer/keyboardist Harry Young aka Kong Kasi.

The Mokos are a collective of self-proclaimed “Urban Māori” emerging from the creative heartbeat of Pōneke. Soundscapes born from a rich blend of indigeneity and urbanism, weaving te reo Māori me ōna tikanga through their music in adventurous new ways. With smooth, R&B and Hip-Hop influences, The Mokos pair phatty basslines - Manaaki Tibble, lush keys - Billy Paratene, tight guitar - Kupa Russell-Hall, stanky percussion - Gala Baumfield, all tastefully tied together with the tender and expressive vocals of Lilly Ellison, forming a distinct identity grounded in Aotearoa.

Bri Louise is a singer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist emerging from roots in Gospel and Neo-Soul, and blending effortlessly into Jazz, Hip Hop, and R&B. Bri’s lyrics explore themes of queerness, local frustration, desire, and intimate stories carried by velvet jazz vocals, rich keys, deep basslines, vibrant guitar, fruity woodwinds and warm, wandering grooves. On stage, Bri Louise is joined by Becky Devine (Bass), Theo Thompson (Guitar), Eloise Edmonds (Saxophone), and Jonathan Stolk (Drums) with guest vocalists and horn players selected from the pool of locals jazz talents, creating a live sound charged with talent.

Flatmate (aka yourFlatmate) has a playful curiosity for music, a passion for storytelling and an infectious energy whether that's behind the decks, in the studio or on the dance floor. He builds psychedelic worlds rooted in rhythm and melody; capturing the warmth of the past and exploring the progressive endeavours of the present.

Cob B2B Mon-Day: Producers for over a decade each, Cob and Mon-Day started DJing together in 2022 with their first B2B set. Now living across the hall from each other, they’re always jamming and making music together. Expect drum machine rhythms, raw club energy, and soulful sounds - with original productions, unreleased tracks, and carefully selected groovers.

If you want to support this wonderful whānau and love beautiful music, come along to Dance for Ms Dance at Rogue and Vagabond on the 23rd of May. Doors at 8:30pm. Tickets are Koha (recommendation of $20+.)
